question: Q5: User Experience vs Monetization

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve tested free trials.
sections.hint: Giving premium features for a few days before asking the user to pay
no_hands: What keeps you from trying free trials?
Do you think they wouldn’t fit your app, or just too complex to set up?
If you added one tomorrow, how long would you make it?
hands_up: How did users respond to the trial?
What patterns did you see in conversions or cancellations?
What problems came up (abuse, tech issues)?
follow_ups: Did anyone see trials harm the app instead of help?
Who tested different lengths, and what did you learn?
What’s one tip to improve trial conversions?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve done A/B testing on paywalls.
sections.hint: Comparing two versions of your pricing or upgrade screen
no_hands: Why haven’t you tested paywalls — too few users, or tools too complex?
If you could test one change tomorrow, what would it be?
hands_up: What did you test, and what happened?
What surprised you about user reactions?
What was your most effective change?
What changes didn’t matter at all?
follow_ups: Who got unexpected results from a very small tweak?
Who thinks testing paywalls isn’t worth the effort?
What’s one paywall design trick that works for you?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve tried upgrade reminders or discounts.
sections.hint: Popups, banners, or limited-time offers that encourage users to upgrade or buy
no_hands: Why don’t reminders or discounts make sense for your apps?
Do you worry they harm user experience?
hands_up: What kind of reminder or discount did you test?
Did it boost conversions or only short-term results?
What was hardest about timing or design?
follow_ups: Did anyone remove reminders because users disliked them?
Who saw strong results in one region but not another?
What balance works between offers and UX?

wrap_up: Where do you draw the line between good UX and monetization?
What monetization design improved UX instead of hurting it?
What design hurt retention even if it looked good?
What’s one rule you’d give new Flutter devs about UX + monetization?
